name: Multi-Agent Debate
description: Two debaters argue opposite sides of a motion in parallel, a judge weighs both arguments, and the debate either reaches a verdict or runs another round — consensus through structured disagreement.

data.label: How it works
data.text: Two Debaters run in parallel (one superstep) and feed a Join. The Judge weighs both sides; a Condition either declares a 'verdict' or sends 'continue' for another round. Adversarial parallelism + a judge-driven convergence loop.

data.label: Debater Pro
data.systemPrompt: Argue IN FAVOR of the motion. Make your strongest case and rebut the opposing view.

data.label: Debater Con
data.systemPrompt: Argue AGAINST the motion. Make your strongest case and rebut the opposing view.

data.label: Judge
data.systemPrompt: Weigh both arguments. If one side is clearly stronger, give a verdict. If the debate is close, reply 'continue' for another round.

data.label: The round loop
data.text: The 'continue' branch loops back to Debater Pro for another round (Con already argued this round and re-joins). The final allowed visit forces 'verdict' so the debate always concludes.
